county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Cottle County formed 1876 from Fannin. County seat is Paducah. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Paducah. Rolling Plains county organized 1892. Ranching heritage. Check Fannin for pre-organization records. Sparse early population. Cottle County Historical Commission.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Cottle County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Paducah holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Cottle County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1876 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Cottle County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1876. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Cottle County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1876. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Cottle County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1876 to 1950 for Cottle County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Native American Records -- Cottle County Area (Comanche and Kiowa)
source_profiles.repository: NARA, Fort Worth / Oklahoma Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-settlement and frontier-era records for Comanche and Kiowa in the Cottle County area. Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Cottle County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Cottle County from 1876. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Crane County formed 1887 from Tom Green. County seat is Crane. West Texas / Permian Basin region with ranching, oil, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Crane. West Texas county organized 1927. Permian Basin oil county. Castle Gap (historic frontier landmark). Check Tom Green for pre-organization records. One of the last Texas counties organized.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Crane County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1887; County Clerk in Crane holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Crockett County formed 1875 from Bexar. County seat is Ozona. West Texas / Permian Basin region with ranching, oil, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Ozona. West Texas ranching county organized 1891. Crockett County Museum. Named for Davy Crockett. Ranching records (sheep and goat). Check Bexar for pre-organization records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Culberson County formed 1911 from El Paso. County seat is Van Horn. West Texas / Permian Basin region with ranching, oil, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Van Horn. Trans-Pecos county created 1911 from El Paso. Guadalupe Mountains area. Southern Pacific Railroad records. Check El Paso County for pre-1911 records. Very sparse population.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Dallam County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Dalhart. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Dalhart. Northwest Panhandle county organized 1891. XIT Ranch territory (one of the largest ranches in history) — records at Panhandle-Plains Historical Museum. Check Bexar for pre-organization records. XIT Museum in Dalhart.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Dallam County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Dalhart holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Dallas County formed 1846 from Nacogdoches, Robertson. County seat is Dallas. North Texas / Blackland Prairie region with farming and urban growth heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Dallas. Major urban county. Dallas Historical Society and Hall of State. Dallas Municipal Archives. Peter's Colony records at TSLAC. African American history at South Dallas Cultural Center. Dealey Fifth Floor Museum. Strong newspaper records (Dallas Morning News from 1885). Significant immigrant communities (German, Jewish, Mexican, Czech).
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Dallas County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1846; County Clerk in Dallas holds deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1846. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Dallas County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Dallas County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Dallas County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Dallas County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Dallas County from 1846. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Lamesa. South Plains county organized 1905. Dawson County Historical Commission. Farming (cotton) and oil. Check Bexar for pre-organization records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: DeWitt County formed 1846 from Gonzales, Goliad, Victoria. County seat is Cuero. Gulf Coast region with port, plantation, and petrochemical heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Cuero. South-central Texas. DeWitt County Historical Museum. DeWitt Colony (Green DeWitt's empresario colony, 1825-1836) — records at GLO and TSLAC. Significant German immigration. Cuero Turkey Trot heritage. Check Gonzales, Goliad, Victoria for pre-1846 records. Sutton-Taylor Feud records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for DeWitt County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1846; County Clerk in Cuero holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: DeWitt County Deed Records -- County Clerk

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1846. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: DeWitt County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for DeWitt County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). DeWitt County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.source_name: DeWitt County German Church Records (Lutheran, Catholic, Methodist)
source_profiles.repository: Local congregations / TSLAC / Texas Lutheran University archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German immigrant church records including baptisms, confirmations, marriages, and burials. DeWitt County has significant German settlement heritage. German-language records may require translation. Check local Lutheran, Catholic, and Methodist congregations.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- DeWitt County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for DeWitt County from 1846. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Deaf Smith County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Hereford. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Hereford. Panhandle county organized 1890. Deaf Smith County Museum. Named for Erastus 'Deaf' Smith, Texas Revolution hero. Farming and ranching (cattle feedlots). Check Bexar for pre-organization records.
